- Gold climbs on safe-haven bids as Middle East tensions escalate. Spot gold traded around $5,362.90/oz (04:52 GMT) as investors sought safety amid US-Israel strikes on Iran. ([dawn.com](https://www.dawn.com/news/1977812/gold-extends-gains-as-middle-east-war-prompts-flight-to-safety))
- On the same day, gold later traded higher to about $5,388.59/oz (12:55 GMT) as strikes and risk-off sentiment persisted. ([brecorder.com](https://www.brecorder.com/news/40409810/gold-prices-jump-as-strikes-on-iran-trigger-rush-to-safe-havens?utm_source=openai))
- Another major take: gold dipped earlier in the session as the dollar strengthened, with prices around $5,049/oz (10:20 a.m. ET), highlighting near-term volatility and a strong dollar headwind. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/conormurray/2026/03/03/gold-silver-prices-plunge-as-iran-conflict-sparks-inflation-concerns-strengthens-dollar/?utm_source=openai))

- U.S. data calendar for March 4 includes ADP Nonfarm Employment Change (13:15), ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI and components (15:00), and the Fed Beige Book (19:00), all of which can influence near-term gold moves. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Economy/Events context from the day-by-day calendar
- March 4, 2026: SNB News Conference (9:30 UTC) and Fed Beige Book (19:00 UTC) are listed in the current-week calendar; ISM services data also on the slate that day. These events are visible on the MQL5 economic calendar page. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- The same calendar shows other March 4 items such as S&P Global PMI series and U.S. ISM Non-Manufacturing metrics that can affect risk appetite and gold pricing. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Note: Current price you gave for XAUUSD is 5124.59.

Central bank announcements (within window)
- SNB News Conference scheduled for March 4, 2026 at 09:30 UTC. Source: SNB official events page. ([snb.ch](https://www.snb.ch/en/services-events/digital-services/webtv?utm_source=openai))
- Fed Beige Book release noted for March 4, 2026 (2:00 p.m. local-ish release timing cited in calendars; U.S. Beige Book summarises current economic conditions). Source: Federal Reserve pages and calendar references. ([federalreserve.gov](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/publications/beige-book-default.htm?utm_source=openai))

Analyst forecasts (contextual, around early March 2026)
- Goldman Sachs raised its end-2026 gold price forecast to $5,400/oz (reported in Reuters-related channels in January 2026; widely cited in market outlets around this period). This provides a backdrop for near-term upside expectations. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/factboxgoldman-sachs-raises-2026end-gold-price-forecast-to-5400oz-4459246?utm_source=openai))
- JP Morgan raised its long-term gold price forecast to around $4,500/oz (late February 2026). This is part of a broader set of bullish calls from major banks on gold in 2026. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/economy-news/factboxjp-morgan-raises-longterm-gold-price-forecast-15-to-4500-an-ounce-4523574?utm_source=openai))
- ING Think (via Capital.com roundup, early March 2026) projects a multi-quarter path in gold with a 2026 average seen around the mid-to-high $4,000s, trending higher as central banks diversify into gold. This helps frame near-term risk-on/risk-off dynamics for XAUUSD. ([capital.com](https://capital.com/en-int/market-updates/gold-price-forecast-03-03-2026?utm_source=openai))

Notes on sources
- Key gold-price events on March 3–4, 2026 are reported by Reuters and Reuters-derived outlets (e.g., Marketscreener and DAWN republishing Reuters copy). Examples include: 5,362.90/oz at 04:52 GMT (Mar 3) and 5,388.59/oz at 12:55 GMT (Mar 3). ([dawn.com](https://www.dawn.com/news/1977812/gold-extends-gains-as-middle-east-war-prompts-flight-to-safety))
- The March 4 calendar items and times come directly from the MQL5 economic calendar page (and SNB’s own event listings). ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Sentiment
- Mixed-to-bullish longer term: safe-haven demand supported higher gold levels during risk spikes, but near-term moves were choppy due to a stronger dollar and evolving geopolitical news. Market chatter around central-bank demand for gold and elevated energy tensions suggests a risk-balanced environment with potential for further upside if the conflict persists or widens. Current price impression around the mid-$5,000s aligns with that risk premium, but intraday reversals are plausible if dollar strength returns or if there’s any de-escalation in the Middle East tensions. ([dawn.com](https://www.dawn.com/news/1977812/gold-extends-gains-as-middle-east-war-prompts-flight-to-safety))

AI RECOMMENDATION: NEUTRAL
Reasoning: The windowed news mix (strong safe-haven bid in early Mar, followed by dollar strength pressure) plus important upcoming calendar events (SNB press conference, Fed Beige Book, ADP/ISM on Mar 4) creates a volatile but structurally supportive backdrop for gold. Bullish targets from major banks exist (e.g., Goldman Sachs at $5,400 end-2026; JP Morgan around $4,500 long-term), but immediate price action shows pullbacks are possible when USD rallies or geopolitical headlines shift. The balance of signals today favors cautious positioning rather than a clear directional bet in the next 24–48 hours. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/factboxgoldman-sachs-raises-2026end-gold-price-forecast-to-5400oz-4459246?utm_source=openai))

## News Summary
- GBPUSD fell to around 1.3300 on March 3, 2026 as Middle East tensions boosted the USD and BoE rate-cut odds collapsed (from about 75% to 28%). This marked a risk-off intraday move for Cable. ([fxstreet.com](https://www.fxstreet.com/amp/news/gbp-usd-slides-to-13300-as-middle-east-tensions-lift-us-dollar-202603031550/?utm_source=openai))
- The pound briefly hit a 2026 low near $1.33 amid broader risk-off and geopolitical headlines, with analysts noting the rapid shift in BoE cut expectations. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/business/live/2026/mar/03/spring-forecast-rachel-reeves-statement-economic-plan-obr-oil-gas-stock-markets-live-updates?utm_source=openai))
- UK investor sentiment deteriorated as fears that Iran-related developments could slow growth pushed UK borrowing costs higher and pressured gilts, weighing on sterling. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/mar/03/uk-borrowing-costs-jump-again-on-fears-iran-conflict-will-curb-growth?utm_source=openai))
- UK economic calendar and central-bank signals for the window include the Spring Forecast Statement for GBP on March 3, 2026 at 10:00 (part of the UK Spring Forecast) which provides the government’s near-term outlook. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- On March 4, 2026, upcoming or released GBP-related macro data include S&P Global/CIPS Services PMI and S&P Global/CIPS Composite PMI for the UK (scheduled around 09:30). These PMIs are key near-term drivers for GBP direction. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- The U.S. side includes the Fed Beige Book release on March 4, 2026 (evening local time), which can influence USD strength and GBPUSD moves through risk sentiment and rate expectations. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Analyst outlook: MUFG published a March 3, 2026 note forecasting GBPUSD around 1.37 in 12 months, underscoring divergent policy paths and a bullish medium-term view for the dollar vs the pound in the near term. ([exchangerates.org.uk](https://www.exchangerates.org.uk/news/45385/2026-03-03-mufg-pound-to-dollar-forecast-gbp-usd-see-1-37-in-12-months-timeframe.html?utm_source=openai))

Notes:
- Current price cited by you: 1.3330899999999999. The day’s reporting alongside the above items shows GBPUSD behavior around the 1.33 level amid geopolitical and policy-event risk. ([fxstreet.com](https://www.fxstreet.com/amp/news/gbp-usd-slides-to-13300-as-middle-east-tensions-lift-us-dollar-202603031550/?utm_source=openai))

## Sentiment
- The market mood in the window appears risk-off for the pound, with safe-haven demand lifting the dollar and market expectations for UK rate cuts pulling back. The combination of Middle East-related risk, lower BoE cut odds, and upcoming UK PMI data has kept GBP under pressure near 1.33. In the near term, sentiment remains cautious to negative for GBP, though PMI releases could spark volatility in either direction.

## AI RECOMMENDATION: BEARISH
**Reasoning**: 
- Multiple pieces of news within the window point to a risk-off stance for GBP: the GBPUSD slide to around 1.3300 on March 3 as BoE rate-cut odds tumbled, and a broader move into safe-haven USD due to geopolitical tensions. This environment tends to weigh on sterling in the immediate term. ([fxstreet.com](https://www.fxstreet.com/amp/news/gbp-usd-slides-to-13300-as-middle-east-tensions-lift-us-dollar-202603031550/?utm_source=openai))
- UK-specific factors (Iran-related macro risk, higher gilt yields, and falling rate-cut expectations) support a weaker pound in the near term. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/mar/03/uk-borrowing-costs-jump-again-on-fears-iran-conflict-will-curb-growth?utm_source=openai))
- While the March 4 S&P Global/CIPS PMIs for the UK are due and could surprise, the prevailing narrative in the window was risk-off and policy-dovishness bets weighing on GBP, with a major long-term forecast (MUFG) still skewing to a weaker pound in the near term. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

**Confidence Level**: MEDIUM

If you’d like, I can pull direct quotes from each article and format a quick one-page briefing with explicit timestamps for each item.

At the same time, ongoing central-bank communications (including SNB events) can inject volatility and affect risk sentiment, potentially limiting clear directional moves in USDJPY.
